programs.name: Health First Colorado Buy-In (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These Medicare Savings Programs pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and co-pays for low-income seniors. QMB covers Part A/B premiums and more; SLMB/QI cover Part B premiums.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 65+ on Medicare with low income/assets (QMB: 100% FPL, $2,000 assets; SLMB: 120% FPL; QI: 135% FPL).
programs.benefit_value: QMB: Full Medicare cost-sharing ($0 premiums/copays); SLMB: Part B premium (~$174.70/month); QI: partial premium.

programs.name: Colorado Medicaid HCBS Waivers (e.g., Home Care Allowance)
programs.what_it_does: These waivers provide home and community-based services to help seniors avoid nursing homes. The Home Care Allowance pays for personal care, respite, home modifications, and supplies up to $1,500/month.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+ with income ≤300% Federal Benefit Rate (~$3,200/month individual), assets ≤$2,000, needing nursing home level care.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $1,500/month for HCA; other waivers vary by services approved.

programs.name: Colorado PACE
programs.what_it_does: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE) offers comprehensive medical and social services in community settings. It includes adult day health, transportation, home care, and covers all Medicare/Medicaid services.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 55+ certified for nursing home level care, able to live safely in community, enrolled in Medicare/Medicaid.
programs.benefit_value: Full coverage of approved services at no additional cost beyond premiums.

programs.name: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: SNAP provides monthly benefits via EBT card for purchasing groceries. It frees up income for other senior care needs.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 60+ with gross income ≤130% FPL (~$1,730/individual), net income test, assets ≤$3,750.

programs.name: LEAP (Low-energy Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: LEAP provides one-time annual payments to help with home heating costs during winter. It covers a portion of energy bills for eligible households.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 60+ with income ≤60% state median (~$3,448/individual), facing heating crisis.
programs.benefit_value: Average $500-1,000 per household; up to $1,200 max.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: WAP funds energy efficiency upgrades like insulation, sealing, and furnace repairs to reduce utility bills. Services are provided at no cost to eligible homes.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ (household income ≤200% FPL), often prioritized via senior centers or energy assistance referrals.
programs.benefit_value: $4,000-7,000 average per home in improvements; lifetime energy savings ~$4,400.

programs.name: Colorado SHIP (State Health Insurance Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: SHIP offers free, unbiased counseling on Medicare options, appeals, and extra help. Counselors help select plans and understand costs.
programs.who_its_for: All Medicare beneficiaries 65+, caregivers, and family; no income limits.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling; potential savings of thousands via optimal plan selection.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels Colorado
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote health and independence. Includes safety checks during delivery.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, often low-income or isolated.
programs.benefit_value: 5-14 meals/week at suggested $4-7/donation; free/subsidized for low-income.

programs.name: Community Access Services (CAS)
programs.what_it_does: CAS provides one-time grants for home repairs, utility aid, and adaptive equipment. It supports aging in place for low-income seniors.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: SCSEP offers part-time paid training jobs in community service to build skills. Leads to unsubsidized employment for older workers.
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income seniors 55+ (income ≤125% FPL), priority to hardest-to-serve.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage pay (~$15-20/hour in CO), 20 hours/week training.

programs.name: Colorado Legal Services (Senior Legal Hotline)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal advice and representation for civil issues like housing, benefits, and abuse. Senior-specific hotline for quick assistance.
programs.benefit_value: Free legal services; resolves issues worth thousands in benefits/housing.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes, assisted living, and similar facilities. Investigates complaints and ensures quality care.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors in long-term care facilities, families, and staff seeking resolution.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy; resolves 80%+ complaints without formal action.

programs.name: Old Age Pension (OAP)
programs.what_it_does: The Colorado Old Age Pension (OAP) is a state-funded cash assistance program that provides monthly payments up to a maximum of $952 (as of 2023) to ensure a minimum income level for eligible seniors, reduced dollar-for-dollar by other income sources. It also offers limited medical benefits for those not qualifying for other coverage.[1][2]
programs.who_its_for: Colorado residents aged 60 or older with income below the maximum benefit level and resources under $2,000 for individuals ($3,000 for couples), who have pursued all other benefits first.[2]
programs.whats_unique: Enshrined in the Colorado Constitution since 1937, OAP is a state-funded safety-net program predating federal SSI and providing cash benefits specifically to seniors 60+, which is not a standard federal program available nationwide.[1][2]
programs.benefit_value: Up to $952 per month (effective 1/1/2023), plus limited medical benefits.[2]

programs.name: Home Care Allowance (HCA)
programs.what_it_does: Part of Colorado's Medicaid HCBS waivers under Health First Colorado, HCA pays for personal care, respite care, home modifications, and supplies to help seniors remain at home. It provides flexible funding for non-medical in-home supports.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Individuals aged 65+ with income ≤300% of Federal Benefit Rate (~$3,200/month for individuals) and assets ≤$2,000, requiring nursing facility level of care.[1]
programs.whats_unique: This state-administered Medicaid waiver program offers a cash-like allowance specifically for home care services, which is a Colorado-specific implementation not identically replicated in every state.[1]

programs.name: Older Coloradans Cash Fund
programs.what_it_does: Provides one-time grants to low-income seniors for essential needs such as home repairs (e.g., ramps, grab bars), utility assistance, and minor adaptive equipment like walkers or shower chairs. It targets immediate safety and accessibility improvements for aging in place.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Colorado residents aged 60+ with household income ≤200% of Federal Poverty Level.[1]
programs.whats_unique: This is a Colorado-specific state fund offering targeted one-time grants for senior home adaptations and utilities, distinct from standard federal programs like LIHEAP or Medicaid waivers.[1]
